html:
Versico: A Second Membrane Brand Under the Same Corporate Roof as Carlisle

Versico is a name that sometimes confuses building owners who are also seeing Carlisle SynTec quoted elsewhere in the bidding process, since both brands are part of the same parent company, Carlisle Companies, while operating as distinct product lines with their own manufacturing and distribution.

Versico's Position in the Market

Versico Roofing Systems is a commercial roofing membrane manufacturer based in Carlisle, Pennsylvania, operating as a subsidiary within the Carlisle Companies portfolio alongside Carlisle SynTec. The two brands maintain separate product identities and distribution channels even though they share corporate ownership, so a bid referencing Versico is referencing a distinct product catalog, not simply a relabeled Carlisle product.

VersiWeld TPO in the Hail Belt

Versico's TPO product line is marketed under the VersiWeld name, covering standard commercial mil thicknesses for mechanically-attached and fully-adhered TPO systems. As with any TPO regardless of manufacturer, mil thickness within this specific product line, not the brand name, is what determines the membrane's puncture and hail impact resistance relevant to this market's storm exposure. Fastening pattern and attachment method still need to be sized to the specific building's wind zone and roof height, since a mismatched fastening density can undercut even a well-rated membrane's wind uplift performance regardless of which manufacturer supplied the sheet.

VersiGard EPDM for Springfield Buildings

Versico also manufactures EPDM membrane under the VersiGard name, in both black and white surface options and in the mil thicknesses relevant to standard commercial applications. The same seam-method consideration that applies to any EPDM product applies here: factory seam-taped systems perform more reliably through the Ozarks' repeated freeze-thaw cycling than older liquid-adhesive seaming methods. Black VersiGard suits unconditioned warehouse space where the added winter heat gain can offset heating cost, while the white option is worth weighing on conditioned office or medical buildings where summer cooling load is the bigger factor.

  • VersiWeld TPO membrane in standard commercial mil thicknesses
  • VersiGard EPDM membrane in black and white surface options
  • VersiFlex PVC membrane products for chemical and grease-resistant applications
  • Fleece-backed membrane options for fully-adhered recover assemblies
  • Insulation and cover board products manufactured to pair with their membrane lines

VersiFlex PVC Where Chemistry Matters

Versico's PVC line, marketed as VersiFlex, addresses the same chemical and grease-resistance use case as any manufacturer's PVC product: rooftop kitchen exhaust, food service exposure, and industrial chemical contact where TPO or EPDM would degrade prematurely. Seam performance on this product depends on the same weld-quality and probing discipline that applies to any heat-welded PVC membrane, which we hold to the identical probing standard we use on TPO and standard PVC jobs, regardless of which manufacturer's sheet is on the roof.

Why the Carlisle-Versico Relationship Comes Up in Bidding

Because both brands sit under the same corporate parent, some contractors carry certification or distribution relationships with one brand but not the other, which can influence which manufacturer shows up in a given bid regardless of whether one product is actually better suited to the building than the other. It's worth asking directly why a specific brand was chosen for a project rather than assuming the choice reflects a technical evaluation of the building's needs. That question isn't a knock on either brand, since both manufacture genuinely comparable membrane products, but an owner comparing two bids that reference different brands under the same parent company should know they're not necessarily looking at two independently engineered systems. We'll quote whichever brand fits the project best, and we'll say so plainly if two competing bids referencing Carlisle and Versico both land on essentially the same membrane spec dressed up under different names.

Reading a Versico-Specified Bid Correctly

As with any manufacturer, a bid that simply states VersiWeld TPO without naming mil thickness and attachment method isn't giving enough detail to compare fairly against a competing proposal. We name the specific product line, gauge, and attachment method explicitly whenever a manufacturer is specified.

Warranty Considerations

Versico's warranty programs, like other major manufacturers, are generally structured around matched, approved system components installed to their specifications. Confirming the specific warranty tier and required component list is worth doing directly for any project where warranty coverage factors into the material decision, particularly on a larger distribution or industrial building where the cost of a denied claim years down the road would be substantial. A substituted fastener, an unapproved insulation swap, or a flashing detail that deviates from the manufacturer's published assembly can each independently affect eligibility, even when the field membrane itself is a genuine Versico product installed by a competent crew.

Questions on Versico Roofing Products

Is Versico the same as Carlisle SynTec?

They share a corporate parent, Carlisle Companies, but operate as separate product brands with their own catalogs and distribution.

Is VersiWeld TPO a lower-cost alternative to Carlisle Sure-Weld TPO?

Pricing varies by market conditions and specific product selected rather than one brand being categorically cheaper, so this should be compared directly on the specific project.

Does VersiGard EPDM use seam tape or liquid adhesive?

Both seaming methods exist across the EPDM product category broadly; we specify factory seam-taped systems regardless of which manufacturer's EPDM is used.

Can Versico products be mixed with another manufacturer's insulation?

They can be installed that way, but doing so may affect eligibility for a Versico system warranty, which typically requires matched, approved components.

Is VersiFlex PVC suitable for a restaurant rooftop exhaust environment?

Yes, PVC as a membrane category is generally suited to grease and chemical exposure, consistent with the performance characteristics of PVC products broadly.
